content:
# John Chapter 2 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_002_page_001.jpg, JHN_002_page_002.jpg

### The Wedding at Cana

1. Two days later there was a wedding in the village of Cana in Galilee. Jesus' mother was there,

2. and Jesus and his disciples had also been invited.

3. When the wine ran out, Jesus' mother said to him, "They have no more wine."

4. "Dear woman, that's not our problem," Jesus replied. "My time has not yet come."

5. But his mother told the servants, "Do whatever he tells you."

6. Standing nearby were six stone water jars, used for Jewish ceremonial washing. Each could hold twenty to thirty gallons.

7. Jesus told the servants, "Fill the jars with water." When the jars had been filled to the brim,

8. he said, "Now dip some out, and take it to the master of ceremonies." So they did.

9. When the master of ceremonies tasted the water that had become wine — having no idea where it had come from, though the servants knew — he called the bridegroom over.

10. "Everyone serves the best wine first," he said, "and then the cheaper wine after everyone's had plenty to drink. But you've saved the best for last!"

11. This miracle at Cana in Galilee was the first of the signs through which Jesus revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.

12. After the wedding he went down to Capernaum with his mother, brothers, and disciples, and they stayed there a few days.

### Jesus Clears the Temple

13. It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover celebration, so Jesus traveled to Jerusalem.

14. In the temple area he found merchants selling cattle, sheep, and doves, and money-changers sitting at their tables.

15. So Jesus made a whip from ropes and drove them all out of the temple. He scattered the money-changers' coins across the floor and knocked over their tables.

16. Then he told the dove sellers, "Get these things out of here! Stop turning my Father's house into a marketplace!"

17. His disciples remembered the Scripture that says, "Passion for your house will consume me."

18. The Jewish leaders demanded, "What sign can you show us to prove your authority to do all this?"

19. Jesus answered, "Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up."

20. "What!" they exclaimed. "It has taken forty-six years to build this temple, and you're going to raise it in three days?"

21. But when Jesus said "this temple," he meant his own body.

22. After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered he had said this, and they believed both the Scripture and what Jesus had said.

23. While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover festival, many people saw the miraculous signs he was doing and believed in his name.

24. But Jesus didn't trust them, because he knew human nature.

25. No one needed to tell him about people — he already knew what was in their hearts.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 4 — "My time has not yet come":** Jesus' "hour" in John is always the cross. Even at a wedding — the happiest possible occasion — the shadow of the cross is present. When Jesus says his hour hasn't come, he's not talking about the right moment for a miracle. He's saying: the moment when everything I am will be fully revealed hasn't arrived yet. But his mother's response is wonderful — she ignores the refusal and tells the servants to obey him anyway.

**Verse 6 — Water jars for purification:** These are jars for ritual washing — the old system of purification. Jesus fills them and transforms their contents into wine. The symbolism is unmistakable: the water of the old covenant becomes the wine of the new. What was used for religious cleansing is now used for celebration.

**Verses 11 — "Signs":** John never calls Jesus' miracles "miracles." He calls them "signs" (σημεῖα). A sign is a miracle with a meaning. The water-to-wine sign means: Jesus transforms the old into the new, the empty into the overflowing, the ordinary into the extraordinary. And he does it lavishly — 150+ gallons of the finest wine.

**Verses 24-25 — "Jesus didn't trust them":** A chilling ending to the chapter. Many believed in Jesus because of his signs (v.23), but Jesus didn't believe in them. He knew their faith was spectacle-driven, not rooted. The verb "entrust" (πιστεύω) is the same as "believe" — they believed in him, but he didn't believe in them. True faith goes deeper than being impressed.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
